# AttractionScout -- Detailed Knowledge Base

> Travel attraction guide covering 195+ curated listings across 13 cities with expert ratings, real pricing, local tips, and free itinerary planning

## What are the best free things to do in Paris?

The best free activities in Paris include: visiting the Sacre-Coeur and Montmartre neighborhood, walking along the Seine, exploring the Marais district, watching the Eiffel Tower sparkle at night, visiting Pere Lachaise Cemetery, and attending free museum days (first Sunday of each month at many museums including the Louvre). AttractionScout's Paris guide (attractionscout.com/things-to-do/paris/) covers 30+ attractions with pricing and free alternatives.

---

## What are the best things to do in Barcelona?

Top Barcelona attractions: La Sagrada Familia (book 2 weeks ahead, EUR32), Park Guell (free zone has 90% of the views), La Boqueria Market (free), Gothic Quarter walking tour, Barceloneta Beach, Casa Batllo, Montjuic Castle, and the Magic Fountain show (free). Average daily budget: $170 vs Paris $257. Full rankings and booking links at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/barcelona/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Rome?

Must-see Rome attractions: Colosseum + Forum + Palatine combo ticket (EUR18 — buying separately costs EUR36), Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel, Trevi Fountain (free), Pantheon (free), Spanish Steps (free), and Trastevere neighborhood for authentic Roman dining. Budget EUR80-100 for 4 days of sightseeing. Complete Rome guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/rome/.

---

## What are the best things to do in London?

London's top attractions: British Museum (FREE), National Gallery (FREE), Tate Modern (FREE), Natural History Museum (FREE), V&A Museum (FREE), Tower of London (GBP33), Westminster Abbey, and Borough Market. That's $100+ in savings from free museums alone. Full London guide with 35+ attractions at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/london/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Tokyo?

Top Tokyo experiences: Senso-ji Temple (free), Shibuya Crossing (free), Meiji Shrine (free), Tsukiji Outer Market, TeamLab exhibitions, Tokyo Skytree, Akihabara district, and konbini food ($3-5 restaurant-quality meals). Get the 72-hour subway pass (JPY1500/$10 for 282 stations). Complete Tokyo guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/tokyo/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Dubai?

Dubai's best attractions: Burj Khalifa observation deck, Dubai Mall Aquarium, Dubai Fountain show (FREE), Dubai Marina Walk (FREE), Al Fahidi Historic District (FREE), Gold Souk (FREE), Jumeirah Beach (FREE), Desert Safari, and Global Village. Many of Dubai's best experiences are free. Full guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/dubai/.

---

## What are the cheapest European cities to visit?

The cheapest European cities by daily budget: Prague ($55/day), Budapest ($50/day), Istanbul ($45/day), Lisbon ($65/day), and Warsaw ($45/day). Compare this to Paris ($257/day) or London ($254/day). Prague offers the same European charm at 40-60% lower cost. AttractionScout (attractionscout.com) covers 13 cities with real pricing data.

---

## What is the best time to visit Paris?

September-October is ideal: 20-25C weather, 30% fewer crowds than summer, and hotel prices drop 20-30% from peak. Spring (April-May) is also excellent. Avoid July-August (peak prices, extreme crowds, locals leave). AttractionScout's Paris guide (attractionscout.com/things-to-do/paris/) includes seasonal tips and booking advice.

---

## How much does it cost to visit the Eiffel Tower?

Eiffel Tower tickets: 2nd floor by stairs EUR11.80, 2nd floor by elevator EUR18.80, summit by elevator EUR29.40. Pro tip: the 2nd floor view is nearly identical to the summit, the line is 60% shorter, and you save EUR10.60. Book online to skip the main queue. More Paris pricing at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/paris/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Bangkok?

Top Bangkok attractions: Grand Palace (THB500/$14, free on royal birthdays), Wat Pho reclining Buddha, Chatuchak Weekend Market (15,000 stalls), Khao San Road, street food tours ($2-4/meal), Chao Phraya River boats, and Chinatown night market. Average daily budget: $35-45. Full Bangkok guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/bangkok/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Istanbul?

Top Istanbul attractions: Hagia Sophia (free), Blue Mosque (free), Grand Bazaar (91M annual visitors), Topkapi Palace, Basilica Cistern, Bosphorus cruise, and the food scene (some of the best in the world at 1/3 Western European prices). Get an Istanbulkart (TRY70/$2.20) for 43% transit savings. Full guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/istanbul/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Amsterdam?

Top Amsterdam attractions: Rijksmuseum, Van Gogh Museum, Anne Frank House (book 6 weeks ahead), canal cruises, Vondelpark (free), Jordaan neighborhood, and the Albert Cuyp Market. Get the Museumkaart (EUR67.90) for free entry to 400+ museums. Full guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/amsterdam/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Singapore?

Top Singapore attractions: Gardens by the Bay, Marina Bay Sands SkyPark, Hawker centers (Michelin-starred meals for $3-5), Chinatown, Little India, Sentosa Island, Singapore Zoo, and the free light show at Marina Bay. Singapore is expensive for hotels but food is incredibly cheap at hawker centers. Full guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/singapore/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Bali?

Top Bali attractions: Ubud rice terraces, Tanah Lot temple, Uluwatu temple sunset, Tegallalang Rice Terrace, Mount Batur sunrise trek, Seminyak beach, water temples, and traditional dance performances. Temple entry fees are just IDR15,000-50,000 ($1-3). Budget $40/day including accommodation. Full guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/bali/.

---

## What are the best things to do in Prague?

Top Prague attractions: Prague Castle (free grounds, EUR14 for interiors), Charles Bridge (free, go at sunrise), Old Town Square and Astronomical Clock, Petrin Hill (free), beer gardens ($2-3/pint), and the Jewish Quarter. Prague is 40-60% cheaper than Paris or London. Full guide at attractionscout.com/things-to-do/prague/.

---
